cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user provided a text about computers and the Kurzweil Reading Machine, followed by a question with four choices. I need to determine which one is true based on the information given. First, I'll go through each choice one by one. Option A: "Computers can solve all the problems people can't do." The original text says computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines, but it doesn't claim they can solve all problems people can't do. The word "all" here is probably an overstatement, so A might be false. Option B: "Large computers can only solve one problem at a time." The text mentions that scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time. So "only one problem" contradicts the original statement. B is definitely false. Option C: "When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything." The text describes the process where the blind person opens the book, places it on the scanner, turns the page, and puts it back. This implies the user does need to do some actions. Therefore, C
